title: Optimizing DFFs whose initial value prevents their value from changing
body:
I'm pretty new to the Yosys codebase so this is only a proof of concept implementation which invokes SAT solver to optimize-away DFFs in the cases that boil down:

````
module top(
    input  clk,
    input  a,
    output b
);
  
    reg b_reg;
    initial begin
        b_reg <= 0;
    end

    assign b = b_reg;
    always @(posedge clk) begin
        b_reg <= a && b_reg;
    end
endmodule
````

Here, the b_reg initial value of 0 is preventing the register value from ever changing. I used SAT solver to check for these situations since expressions for the DFF next value can be more complex and end up in this simple form only after minimization.

I invoked the SAT solver using string command via Pass::call, since it seemed that I would need to either copy or refactor a lot of the code from the ``passes/sat/sat.cc`` module. The generated SAT problem for the example above is:

 ``sat -ignore_unknown_cells -prove $0\b_reg[0:0][0] 0 -set b_reg[0] 0``

So for the multi-bit registers, the above check would be run for each of the individual bits. If the SAT solver proves that a portion of the register cannot change its value, that portion of the register is replaced by a constant.

Again, I'm not familiar with the codebase so I'll be willing to put in some more work into the implementation if this approach makes sense and I could be given some guidelines on how to fit it naturally into Yosys.
